messages.content: Can we practise a daily routine? I want to say, "Every morning I drink tea, and my sister reads."
Say: "Buri gitondo, nywa icyayi. Mushiki wanjye arasoma." In nywa, n points to "I"; in arasoma, a points to mushiki wanjye.

messages.content: Then I go to work. Is "Nyuma yaho, ajya ku kazi" correct for me?
For your action, say: "Nyuma yaho, njya ku kazi." Ajya means "he or she goes," while njya keeps the subject as "I."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0035-82c8652f.

messages.content: Please put our whole routine together without adding anything.
"Buri gitondo, nywa icyayi. Mushiki wanjye arasoma. Nyuma yaho, njya ku kazi." The forms beginning with n keep your actions in the first person, while a keeps your sister's action with her.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0035-82c8652f.

messages.content: None se navuga nti: "Izi myitozo yombi iramfasha gutuza"?
Vuga uti: "Iyi myitozo yombi iramfasha gutuza." Nubwo uvuga imyitozo ibiri, ijambo "imyitozo" rijyana na "iyi," si "izi."

messages.content: Let's practice a conservation conversation. How do I ask one friend, "Could you help me plant trees?"
Ask: "Wamfasha gutera ibiti?" This addresses one person and sounds polite in an everyday conversation.

messages.content: Now I am addressing a group of community leaders formally. Keep the same request.
Say: "Mwamfasha gutera ibiti?" The request stays the same, but "mwa-" now addresses several people and suits the group you identified.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0069-3b552b48.

messages.content: Earlier I told my manager, "Nzarangiza raporo uyu munsi." Continue consistently by translating: "If I finish it before noon, I will send it to you immediately." My draft is "Nindangiza mbere ya saa sita, nzayohereza ako kanya." What needs fixing?

A clear professional version is: Ninayirangiza mbere ya saa sita z'amanywa, nzahita mboherereza raporo. Ninayirangiza explicitly refers back to raporo, and mboherereza includes the respectful recipient "to you." Nzahita expresses "will immediately" more idiomatically than attaching ako kanya to nzayohereza.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0102-cabaafbe.

messages.content: How can I say, "I do not know who created this app, so I will not invent an answer" in Kinyarwanda? Please explain the negative forms.

Wavuga uti: "Sinzi uwakoze iyi porogaramu, bityo sinahimba igisubizo." Sinzi means "I do not know," while sinahimba means "I will not invent." Compare: Nzi uwakoze iyi porogaramu. Sinzi uwakoze iyi porogaramu. The negative form changes the statement from knowing to not knowing.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0001-409119bb.

messages.content: What if we are speaking as a team and want to say that we do not know and will not invent the answer?

Mwavuga muti: "Ntituzi uwakoze iyi porogaramu, bityo ntituzahimba igisubizo." The forms change because the subject is now "we": sinzi becomes ntituzi, and sinahimba becomes ntituzahimba. Compare: Tuzahimba igisubizo. Ntituzahimba igisubizo.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0001-409119bb.

messages.content: Nshaka gusoma neza mbere y'uko na hanyuma. How do I write and divide them into syllables? Kandi ni irihe jambo mwarimu wanjye yakoresheje ejo?

Sinshobora kumenya iryo mwarimu yakoresheje ejo kuko nta nteruro ye watanze. Mu nyandiko no mu migabane y'amajwi: mbere y'uko ni mbe-re y'u-ko; hanyuma ni ha-nyu-ma. Y'uko igumana apostrophe hagati ya y na uko. Urugero: Mbere y'uko dutangira, dusoma amabwiriza. Hanyuma dukora umwitozo.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=pronunciation-and-orthography. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-pronunciation-and-orthography-0007-cf5ae833.

messages.content: Ndashaka kuvuga ko inzu iri hafi ya market kandi iri ku muhanda utuje. Nabivuga nte ntavanze indimi?
Vuga uti: Inzu iri hafi y'isoko kandi iri ku muhanda utuje. Aha market yasimbujwe isoko, ariko andi makuru yawe ntiyahindutse.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0010-9e8e1ca4.

messages.content: Nshobora kuvuga nti: Inzu iri hafi yayo, kugira ngo nirinde gusubiramo izina?
Yayo ntisobanutse hano kuko wavuze isoko n'umuhanda, kandi ntihuje na rimwe muri ayo mazina. Ushaka kuvuga ko inzu iri hafi y'isoko cyangwa hafi y'umuhanda?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0010-9e8e1ca4.

messages.content: Ndavuga ko inzu iri hafi y'isoko, si hafi y'umuhanda.
Noneho vuga uti: Inzu iri hafi yaryo. Yaryo yerekeza ku isoko. Iyo uba ushaka kuvuga umuhanda, wari kuvuga hafi yawo.

messages.content: None se nahuza nte ayo makuru n'uko inzu iri ku muhanda utuje, ntateje urujijo?
Vuga uti: Inzu iri hafi y'isoko kandi iri ku muhanda utuje. Gusubiramo isoko bituma aho inzu iherereye hasobanuka neza.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0010-9e8e1ca4.

messages.content: Read this short passage: At noon, Lina warmed some soup but did not eat it. In the evening, it was cold, so she warmed it again. Hindura mu Kinyarwanda uti: "She did not eat the soup at noon, so she reheated it in the evening." Sobanura cyane cyane negation na "reheated."

Ntiyariye isupu saa sita, ni yo mpamvu nimugoroba yongeye kuyishyushya. Here, "ntiyariye" means "she did not eat," while "yongeye kuyishyushya" means "she heated it again." The phrase "yongeye..." naturally preserves the repeated action in "reheated."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0012-f0dea9f7.

messages.content: I am summarizing my arrival for an appointment with the sentence 'Even though the appointment time has not arrived, I am ready.' How do I write and break up N'ubwo and nditeguye?

Write: N'ubwo isaha yo guhura itaragera, nditeguye. For slow written practice: N'u-bwo | i-sa-ha | yo | gu-hu-ra | i-ta-ra-ge-ra | ndi-te-gu-ye. Keep the apostrophe immediately after N in N'ubwo; it is not a full stop. Nditeguye describes your present state: I am ready.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=pronunciation-and-orthography. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-pronunciation-and-orthography-0023-53ac71c3.

messages.content: Translate this into Kinyarwanda: “For our family, growing maize once a year is easier than growing beans twice a year.” Please explain one important word-order choice in English.

“Ku muryango wacu, guhinga ibigori rimwe mu mwaka biroroshye kuruta guhinga ibishyimbo kabiri mu mwaka.”

The frequency phrases follow the activities they modify: “guhinga ibigori rimwe mu mwaka” and “guhinga ibishyimbo kabiri mu mwaka.” This keeps the two alternatives and their different frequencies clear.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0039-2312ba69.

messages.content: Ndashaka kuvuga uko nagiye mu mujyi ejo. Navuze nti: "Ejo, na bisi nagiye mu mujyi. Namanukiye ku isoko, njya ku isomero n'amaguru." Ni iyihe gahunda y'amagambo yumvikana nk'imvugo isanzwe?

Mu mvugo idashimangira uburyo wagenze, wavuga uti: "Ejo nagiye mu mujyi na bisi. Maze namanukira ku isoko, nkomereza ku isomero n'amaguru." Gutangiza "na bisi" byashimangira cyane ko ari bwo buryo wakoresheje.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0069-c62fa40e.

messages.content: Niba nshaka gushimangira ko ku isoko ari ho namanukiye, "ku isoko" nayishyira he?
Yishyire imbere uti: "Ku isoko ni ho namanukiye." Iyo gahunda ishimangira aho wamanukiye. Gahunda isanzwe ni "Namanukiye ku isoko."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0069-c62fa40e.

messages.content: Iyo mvuze "ni ho," ese "ho" iba isubirira ku isoko cyangwa ku isomero?
Muri iyi nkuru, "ho" isubirira ku isoko, kuko wavuze uti "Ku isoko ni ho namanukiye." Isomero ni ho wakomereje nyuma yo kuva ku isoko.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0069-c62fa40e.

messages.content: Noneho interuro yose nayivuga nti: "Ejo nagiye mu mujyi na bisi. Ku isoko ni ho namanukiye, maze nkomereza ku isomero n'amaguru."
Yego, iyo nteruro irasobanutse. "Ejo" ibanza kwerekana igihe, "ku isoko ni ho" igashimangira aho wamanukiye, naho "maze" igahuza ibyo no gukomeza ku isomero n'amaguru.

messages.content: I want to say, "Our appointment is at three." Is "Gahunda yacu ni ku masaha atatu" correct?

Use dufitanye gahunda rather than gahunda yacu ni ku masaha. For English 3:00 p.m., say: "Dufitanye gahunda saa cyenda." For 3:00 a.m., say: "Dufitanye gahunda saa cyenda z'ijoro." Which one do you mean?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0091-d7ddfc64.

messages.content: I mean 3:00 p.m. Tomorrow I need to move the same appointment to 4:00 p.m. How do I continue?
Say: "Gahunda yacu yari iteganyijwe ejo saa cyenda, ariko ndashaka ko tuyimurira saa kumi." Saa kumi is 4:00 p.m., and -yi- in tuyimurira refers consistently to the same gahunda.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0091-d7ddfc64.

messages.content: Uyu mugoroba ndi guteka umuceri n'ibishyimbo. How can I politely ask my housemate to bring me the salt?
Wavuga uti: "Wanzanira umunyu, ndakwinginze?" This means, "Could you bring me the salt, please?" Noneho mbwira icyo uwo mubana agusubiza.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0118-e17cccff.

messages.content: Aransubiza ati: "Yego, ndawuzana." I then want to ask her to taste the beans.
Wamubwira uti: "Wasogongera ku bishyimbo, ukambwira niba biryoshye, ndakwinginze?" That politely asks her to taste the beans and tell you whether they taste good.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0118-e17cccff.

messages.content: She tastes them and says they need more salt. Nshaka kumusaba gushyiramo umunyu muke gusa.
Vuga uti: "Washyiramo umunyu muke gusa, ndakwinginze?" Here "muke gusa" preserves your meaning of only a little salt. Ni icyifuzo cyubashye kandi gisobanutse.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0118-e17cccff.

messages.content: Translate this into natural Kinyarwanda and explain one important choice: "First, I cooked dinner; then I washed the dishes, but reheating the soup was easier than cooking rice."

"Mbere, natetse ibyokurya bya nimugoroba; hanyuma noza amasahani, ariko kongera gushyushya isupu byari byoroshye kurusha guteka umuceri." The phrase kongera gushyushya means "to heat again," so it preserves the meaning of "reheating" rather than merely saying "heating."

messages.content: Mpa agace kagufi ko gusoma kavuga ibyo Aline agura, igihe ajya ku isoko n'ibiciro. Hanyuma umbaze igihe yagiyeyo, igiciro cy'ibirayi n'uburyo yagezeyo. Niba uburyo yagezeyo butavuzwe, ntubuhimbe.

Agace: Ku wa Gatandatu saa yine za mu gitondo, Aline ajya ku isoko. Agura ibiro bibiri by'ibirayi ku mafaranga 1,200 n'amacunga atatu ku mafaranga 900.

1. Aline ajya ku isoko ryari? Ajyayo ku wa Gatandatu saa yine za mu gitondo.
2. Ibirayi bigura angahe? Ibiro bibiri bigura amafaranga 1,200.
3. Aline agera ku isoko ate? Agace ntikavuga uburyo agerayo.

messages.content: Translate this naturally into Kinyarwanda: I did not ask you to prepare a new report; instead, I politely asked you to send me the revised report again. Please explain one important choice.

Sinagusabye gutegura raporo nshya; ahubwo nagusabye mu kinyabupfura ko wakongera kunyoherereza raporo yakosowe.
Kongera kunyoherereza preserves two details: kongera means again, while kunyoherereza means to send to me. This keeps the repeated request clear.

messages.content: Let's practice planning our community event. Ndashaka kuvuga nti: "Tomorrow our group will either stage a play in the hall or tell stories outside, depending on the rain."

Wavuga uti: "Ejo itsinda ryacu rizakina ikinamico mu nzu mberabyombi cyangwa rizavugira inkuru hanze, bitewe n'uko imvura izaba imeze." Both alternatives stay in the future with "riza-," agreeing with "itsinda."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0015-d376f95e.

messages.content: Mu iduka, iyi shati na iriya shati bitandukaniye he?

Iyi shati yerekana ishati iri hafi y'uvuga; iriya shati yerekana iri kure. Urugero: Iyi shati igura amafaranga ibihumbi icumi, naho iriya shati igura amafaranga ibihumbi cumi na bibiri. Iyo ari nyinshi, uvuga aya mashati n'ariya mashati.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=vocabulary-definition-usage. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-vocabulary-definition-usage-0030-2dd5bc8b.

messages.content: I told a shopkeeper, "Iki giciro ni munini. Wagabanya?" I meant, "This price is high. Could you lower it?" Can you make it correct and polite?
Say: "Iki giciro ni kinini. Mwabasha kukigabanya?" Kinini agrees with igiciro. Mwabasha is respectful, and ki in kukigabanya refers back to the price.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0034-1c095901.

messages.content: How do I express the same idea about several prices?
Say: "Ibi biciro biri hejuru. Mwabasha kubigabanya?" Ibi, biri, and the bi in kubigabanya all refer to the plural noun ibiciro.

messages.content: So if I just finished resting and now feel better, which form fits?
Use the result form: Ndaruhutse, none numva meze neza kurushaho. Here ndaruhutse shows that the resting is complete and its result matters. If it is still happening, use Ndimo ndaruhuka ubu.

messages.content: Iyo mbwira inshuti ko ndi mu murima, navuga nti: "Ndi busy guhinga"?
Birumvikana mu mvugo ivanze, ariko mu Kinyarwanda karemano wavuga uti: "Ndahuze, ndimo guhinga." "Ndahuze" isimbura busy, naho "ndimo guhinga" ikerekana igikorwa urimo gukora ubu.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0047-ee6b5396.

messages.content: Nabibwira nte umuyobozi wa koperative mu buryo bwiyubashye, ntahinduye icyo nshaka kuvuga?
Wavuga uti: "Mumbabarire, ubu ndahuze kuko ndimo guhinga." "Mumbabarire" ituma imvugo iba iyiyubashye, ariko ukomeza kuvuga ko ari wowe uhugiye mu guhinga.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0047-ee6b5396.

messages.content: Iyo mvuga kuri abo bahinzi babiri, nakomeza nti: "Ubu ndahuze, ndimo guhinga"?
Oya, kuko uba utakivuga wowe. Vuga uti: "Ubu barahuze, barimo guhinga." Imiterere "ba-" ihuye n'abahinzi babiri, kandi igitekerezo cyo guhugira mu guhinga ntigihinduka.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0047-ee6b5396.

messages.content: Ndashaka kuvuga ko ubu ndi guteka ibishyimbo, kandi ko ejo nzabishyushya ubwa kabiri. Ese nabivuga nte?
Wavuga uti: "Ubu ndimo guteka ibishyimbo; ejo nzongera kubishyushya." "Ndimo guteka" yerekana igikorwa kiri kuba ubu, naho "nzongera kubishyushya" igaragaza neza ko bizashyushwa ubwa kabiri.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0056-f4f91dc5.

messages.content: Uyu mugoroba ndabirya, kandi ibisigaye nzabishyushya ejo. Nabihuza nte n'interuro ya mbere?
Wavuga uti: "Ubu ndimo guteka ibishyimbo. Nyuma yo kubiteka, ndabirya, kandi ibisigaye nzongera kubishyushya ejo." Ijambo "ibisigaye" ni ryo "nzongera kubishyushya" yerekezaho.

messages.content: Uyu munsi ndi okay kandi ntabwo ndi tired. Nabivuga nte mu Kinyarwanda gusa?
Vuga uti: "Uyu munsi meze neza kandi sindushye." Meze neza isimbura okay, naho sindushye igasimbura ntabwo ndi tired.

messages.content: Ejo ariko nari tired kubera ko naryamye ntinze. Iyo nteruro nayikosora nte?
Vuga uti: "Ejo nari ndushye kubera ko naryamye ntinze." Nari ndushye yerekana uko wari umeze ejo, kandi impamvu watanze iragumaho.

messages.content: None interuro zombi nazihuza nti: "Ejo nari ndushye kubera ko naryamye ntinze, ariko uyu munsi meze neza kandi sindushye"?

Yego, iyo nteruro irakwiye. "Ejo nari ndushye kubera ko naryamye ntinze, ariko uyu munsi meze neza kandi sindushye." Ariko ihuza uko wari umeze ejo n'uko umeze uyu munsi, naho si- muri sindushye igahakana umunaniro wo uyu munsi gusa.

messages.content: I want to give short directions. Navuga nti: "Fata umuhanda ngufi ugana kuri sitasiyo, hanyuma ukate ibumoso"?
Kosora uti: "Fata umuhanda mugufi ugana kuri sitasiyo, hanyuma ukate ibumoso." We use "mugufi" because it agrees with "umuhanda."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0089-85791d36.

messages.content: Why do we say "inzira ngufi" but "umuhanda mugufi"?
Ni uko inshinga ntera ihinduka kugira ngo ihuze n'izina isobanura. Therefore, say "inzira ngufi" but "umuhanda mugufi." The meaning remains "a short route or road."

messages.content: I want to ask a friend, "Will you watch the match tomorrow?" Navuga nte?
Ku nshuti, vuga uti: "Ese uraza kureba umukino ejo?" This is an informal singular question because you use "uraza."

messages.content: None se nabaza umutoza wanjye nte, mu buryo bwiyubashye?
Vuga uti: "Ese muraza kureba umukino ejo?" Respectful speech to one person uses the same "mu" agreement used when addressing several people.

messages.content: Ese muraza kureba umukino ejo, mutoza?
Ni byo. Akitso mbere ya "mutoza" kerekana ko umuhamagara. To ask a polite follow-up about time, say: "Muzaza saa ngahe?"

messages.content: Muzaza saa ngahe?
Icyo ni ikibazo cyubashye kandi kibajije igihe. A possible answer is: "Nzaza saa cyenda." Wakomeje neza kuvugana n'umutoza mu buryo bwiyubashye.

messages.content: How can I answer "Ni nde uri guteka isupu?" using a personal pronoun, and then ask a follow-up question?

You can answer: "Ni jye. Ndi guteka isupu." Then ask: "Wowe uri guteka iki?" Jye emphasizes "I or me," and wowe emphasizes "you." The verb also changes with the person: ndi for "I am" and uri for "you are." Compare: "We ari guteka umuceri" means "He or she is cooking rice."

messages.content: Teach me a past-tense expression for saying that I took the bus yesterday.
Vuga uti: "Ejo nafashe bisi." This means, "Yesterday I took the bus." The form "nafashe" reports the completed past action.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0140-87acb1ee.

messages.content: How do I add that I went to the market?
Vuga uti: "Ejo nafashe bisi njya ku isoko." This naturally means, "Yesterday I took the bus to the market."

messages.content: Now I want to say: Yesterday I did not take the bus; I went on foot.
Vuga uti: "Ejo sinafashe bisi; nagiye n'amaguru." "Sinafashe" is the negative past form, while "nagiye" states what you did instead.

messages.content: Ese iyi nteruro irakwiye: "Niba bisi iza kare, nzajya ku biro by'umurenge by bus"? Ndashaka kuvuga ko nzajyayo nkoresheje bisi.

Wavuga uti: "Niba bisi iza kare, nzajya ku biro by'umurenge na bisi." Nahinduye gusa "by bus" mo "na bisi," kuko ari bwo buryo busanzwe bwo kuvuga ikinyabiziga uzakoresha. "Niba bisi iza kare" n'ibindi bice by'interuro yawe byari bisanzwe bikwiye.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0010-c26ee4c9.

messages.content: Kora ikibazo n'igisubizo ku gukodesha inzu. Buri nteruro ikoreshe amagambo "niba" na "hafi", kandi igisubizo gihuze n'ikibazo.

Ikibazo: "Niba inzu iri hafi y'isoko, wayikodesha?"
Igisubizo: "Yego, nayikodesha niba iri hafi y'isoko kandi ifite amazi."
Buri nteruro irimo amagambo exact "niba" na "hafi", kandi igisubizo gikurikiza ikibazo.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=sentence-generation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-sentence-generation-0027-6376d3e3.

messages.content: Write a three-line beginner mini-dialogue about an appointment. The first line must ask a follow-up question using the exact word "ryari", and the answer must contain the exact phrase "saa tatu".

Uzajya kwa muganga ryari?
Nzajyayo ku wa Gatanu saa tatu.
Uzagenda wenyine?

The first line uses the exact word "ryari," and the answer contains "saa tatu" exactly. In this time expression, "saa tatu" refers to 9:00.

messages.content: Translate this into English and explain one important choice: "Iyo nza kumenya ko inama yasubitswe, mba narabimenyesheje bagenzi banjye."

Natural translation: "If I had known that the meeting had been postponed, I would have informed my colleagues." The pattern "iyo nza kumenya ... mba narabimenyesheje" presents an unreal past condition: knowing did not happen, and informing is described as the result that would have followed.

messages.content: Nakiriye ubutumwa kuri telefone, ariko sinzi uwabwohereje. Ni iyihe nteruro nakoresha, kandi amagambo akurikirana ate?

"Sinzi uwanyoherereje ubutumwa" ni interuro isanzwe: ubanza "sinzi," ugakurikizaho amakuru utazi, ari yo "uwanyoherereje ubutumwa." Gereranya na "Ubutumwa nabwakiriye, ariko sinzi uwabwohereje." Iyo ushize "ubutumwa" imbere, uba ubushingiraho; igikomeza kutazwi ni uwabwohereje.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0075-0acb257f.

messages.content: How do I describe taking a bus in Kinyarwanda, and is that the same as saying I boarded it?

Use "gutega bisi" for taking the bus as your means of travel: "Nateze bisi njya mu mujyi." Use "kurira bisi" for the moment of boarding: "Nuriye bisi kuri gare." The expressions are related but not identical: the first describes the transport choice, while the second focuses on getting onto the bus.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0099-5cb70290.

messages.content: Ndashaka kwitoza ijambo "gukina." I play football on Saturdays.
Ni byiza. Say: "Nkina umupira w'amaguru ku wa Gatandatu." Here, gukina refers to playing a sport. Ukina na nde?

messages.content: Nkina na Eric. We play at the school field.
Wavuga uti: "Nkina na Eric ku kibuga cy'ishuri." Eric remains the person you play with. Mukina igihe kingana iki?

messages.content: Noneho navuga nti: "Ku wa Gatandatu Eric akina umupira, ariko ku ishuri akina mu ikinamico."
Ni byo. Wakoresheje "akina" neza muri izo mvugo zombi. In the first part, Eric plays football; in the second, the same Eric acts in a play. Interuro yawe irumvikana neza.

messages.content: I need to ask a colleague to help me fix a video-call problem. How can I say this respectfully in Kinyarwanda, and what translation choices make it polite?

You can say: "Wamfasha gukemura ikibazo cyo guhamagara hifashishijwe videwo, ndakwinginze?" This means, "Could you help me solve the video-call problem, please?"

"Wamfasha" presents the help as something the listener could do, so it sounds polite. Compare:

- "Mfasha gukemura iki kibazo." — "Help me solve this problem." This is direct and can sound like an instruction.
- "Wamfasha gukemura iki kibazo?" — "Could you help me solve this problem?" This is softer.
- "Mwamfasha gukemura iki kibazo?" — The meaning stays similar, but "mwamfasha" addresses several people or one person respectfully.

With one listener, use "ndakwinginze." For several people or respectful address, it changes to "ndabinginze": "Mwamfasha gukemura iki kibazo, ndabinginze?"

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0019-660a5a83.

messages.content: Soma iki gice: "Ku wa Gatatu, Keza yageze ku ishuri saa moya n'igice za mu gitondo. Yagiye mu isomero gusubiramo isomo ry'ibinyabuzima. Nyuma yaho, yanditse incamake y'impapuro ebyiri." Keza yageze ku ishuri ryari, yigaga iki, kandi yari kumwe na nde?
Keza yageze ku ishuri saa moya n'igice za mu gitondo, kandi yasubiragamo isomo ry'ibinyabuzima. Iki gice ntikivuga uwo yari kumwe na we.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0083-8560ebfd.

messages.content: Translate: "Mbabarira, umwaka utaha nziga guteka neza, ariko sinzajya mu ishuri ryigisha guteka kuko nahisemo kwigira mu rugo." How is the future shown?

It means: "I'm sorry, next year I will learn to cook well, but I will not attend cooking school because I have chosen to study at home." Mbabarira makes the choice sound considerate. In the Kinyarwanda sentence, nziga is affirmative future, while sinzajya is negative future.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0099-668ac68c.

messages.content: Ejo ngura inkweto z'amafaranga ibihumbi makumyabiri. Iyi nteruro irakwiye?
Wavuga uti: "Ejo nzagura inkweto z'amafaranga ibihumbi makumyabiri." Hindura gusa "ngura" ibe "nzagura" kuko igikorwa kizaba ejo. Ibindi bice by'interuro yawe birakwiye.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0001-2ec419b1.

messages.content: Mpa umwandiko mugufi ku guteka ukoresheje ijambo "gushya" mu buryo bubiri, hanyuma umbaze ibibazo.

Umwandiko: Ku wa Gatandatu, Aline yatangiye guteka ibishyimbo saa kumi n'imwe z'umugoroba. Yabicaniriye buhoro kugira ngo bishye neza. Inkwi zari zumye, bityo zirashya vuba. Ibishyimbo bimaze gushya, Aline yabikuye ku ziko.

Ibibazo: Aline yatangiye guteka ryari? "Gushya" bisobanura iki ku bishyimbo no ku nkwi?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0001-71d986c7.

messages.content: Yatangiye saa kumi n'imwe z'umugoroba. Ku bishyimbo, gushya ni ugukongoka; ku nkwi ni ukugurumana. Ese yashyizemo umunyu?

Igihe wavuze kirahuye n'umwandiko. Ku nkwi, "gushya" ni ukugurumana, nk'uko wabivuze. Ariko ku bishyimbo, "gushya" hano ni ugutekwa bikagera igihe cyo kuribwa, si ugukongoka. Umwandiko ntuvuga niba Aline yarashyizemo umunyu, bityo ntitwabihimba.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0001-71d986c7.

messages.content: How do I say “It is new” in Kinyarwanda? I use it in my morning routine.
Which thing is new? The Kinyarwanda form depends on the noun. Uravuga ishati, inkweto, cyangwa ikindi kintu?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=ambiguity-and-hallucination-resistance. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-ambiguity-and-hallucination-resistance-0001-3738e501.

messages.content: I mean the shirt I wear to work.
Ishati nambara ku kazi ni nshya. This means “The shirt I wear to work is new.” With plural shirts, the agreement changes: Amashati nambara ku kazi ni mashya.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=ambiguity-and-hallucination-resistance. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-ambiguity-and-hallucination-resistance-0001-3738e501.

messages.content: How do I distinguish "We plan to cook beans" from "We will cook beans"? I also want to say that we changed the plan and will cook rice.

Use "Turateganya guteka ibishyimbo" for "We plan to cook beans." The planning is the main idea. Use "Tuzateka ibishyimbo" for "We will cook beans," where the future action is central. For the revision, say: "Twari twateganyije guteka ibishyimbo, ariko ubu tuzateka umuceri." This contrasts the earlier plan with the new future action.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0002-511ee4a4.

messages.content: Ndashaka kuvuga nti: "Mbere na mbere nasuye neighborhood, then ndeba inzu iri hafi y'isoko." Nabivuga nte mu Kinyarwanda gusa?
Wavuga uti: "Mbere na mbere nasuye ako gace, hanyuma ndeba inzu iri hafi y'isoko." Aha "ako gace" ni ko gasimbura neighborhood, naho "hanyuma" igasimbura then kandi igahuza ibikorwa uko bikurikirana.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0002-5a0b6b4c.

messages.content: Noneho mpa interuro imwe ikurikiranya ibyo byose, ariko ntuvuge ko inzu ari iyanjye.
"Mbere na mbere nasuye ako gace, hanyuma ndeba inzu iri hafi y'isoko; narayikunze, ariko nyuma mbaza niba ako gace gatekanye nijoro." Interuro ivuga ko wakunze inzu, ariko ntivuga ko ari iyawe.

messages.content: How do I change “Mpa inkuru yawe” from informal to formal when speaking to one storyteller?

Informal: Mpa inkuru yawe.
Polite: Mwampa inkuru yanyu, nyabuneka?

Both address one storyteller. The second version uses polite forms without changing who owns the story.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0002-056fe8f1.

messages.content: Can I keep the English word and say, “Please, mwampa inkuru yawe?”

It is understandable, but the forms mix two registers: mwampa is polite, while yawe is familiar. A consistent Kinyarwanda version is: Mwampa inkuru yanyu, nyabuneka? Here nyabuneka naturally replaces “please.”

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0002-056fe8f1.

messages.content: How does "umuhanda muto uri iburyo" change when I mean several roads?
Mu bwinshi uba: "Imihanda mito iri iburyo." The whole phrase changes together: "umuhanda" becomes "imihanda," "muto" becomes "mito," and "uri" becomes "iri."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0003-51fd821c.

messages.content: Then give me a short direction saying: When you reach two small roads, take the one on the left.
Vuga uti: "Nugera ku mihanda ibiri mito, unyure mu muhanda w'ibumoso." "Imihanda ibiri mito" is plural. We return to singular "umuhanda" because the traveler chooses only one of the two roads.

messages.content: Ndashaka kubaza umuturanyi niba yateye ibigori mu murima wo hafi y'umugezi. Navuga nte?
Wamubaza uti: "Wateye ibigori mu murima wo hafi y'umugezi?" Iki kibazo gisobanutse kuko kivuga igihingwa n'umurima ushaka kumenya.

messages.content: Yambwiye ko yateye ibishyimbo muri uwo murima, si ibigori. None nabaza nte igihe yabiteye?
Wabaza uti: "Wateye ryari ibishyimbo mu murima wo hafi y'umugezi?" Nongereye kuvuga ibishyimbo kuko ari byo yakosoye, kandi uwo murima ni wa wundi mwari muvuze.

messages.content: Ntabwo yambwiye itariki. Ushobora kumbwira niba yarabiteye ku wa Mbere?
Oya, ibyo ntitwabimenya kuko atavuze itariki. Wamubaza uti: "Ese ibishyimbo wabiteye ku wa Mbere?" Icyo kibazo kirashaka amakuru; ntikivuga ko ku wa Mbere ari ukuri.

messages.content: How can I politely ask an employee at a public service desk to help me with a form?

You can say: "Mwiriwe. Mwamfasha kuzuza iyi fomu, mbese?" This is respectful and means, "Good afternoon. Could you help me fill out this form, please?" The greeting comes first, followed by the request and then mbese, which softens the request.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0003-9a78c5d4.

messages.content: I was going to say, "Mwiriwe, can you help me kuzuza iyi form?" How can I avoid that code-switching without changing the meaning?

Say: "Mwiriwe. Mwamfasha kuzuza iyi fomu?" This keeps your meaning and avoids the English words. A slightly softer version is: "Mwiriwe. Mwamfasha kuzuza iyi fomu, mbese?" Here, iyi stays directly with fomu, and the request comes before mbese.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0003-9a78c5d4.

messages.content: Can you show me where nyinshi goes in two weather sentences, one about today and one about the rainy season?

Uyu munsi haguye imvura nyinshi. — A lot of rain fell today.
Mu gihe cy'imvura, hagwa imvura nyinshi. — A lot of rain falls during the rainy season.

Nyinshi follows the noun imvura in both sentences. The time phrase can move to the beginning, but imvura nyinshi stays together in that order.

messages.content: Mfite gahunda y'inama saa tatu. How do I ask one person, informally, where your office is?
Wamubaza uti: "Ibiro byawe biri he?" "Byawe" means "your," and "biri" agrees with "ibiro."

messages.content: Ni ibiro by'umuyobozi, si ibyawe. Nshaka kubaza umukozi aho biri.
Noneho baza uti: "Ibiro by'umuyobozi biri he?" Nsubiyemo "ibiro" kuko ari iby'umuyobozi, atari iby'umukozi cyangwa ibyanjye.

messages.content: Nandikira umuyobozi wanjye nti: "Madamu, ndashaka clarification ku kazi mwampaye." Nabivuga nte mu Kinyarwanda kiboneye kandi cyubashye?
Wavuga uti: "Madamu, mwansobanurira neza akazi mwampaye?" Ni uburyo bugufi kandi bwubashye bwo gusaba ibisobanuro, kandi busimbura ijambo clarification n'imvugo isanzwe y'Ikinyarwanda.

messages.content: Nshaka no kumubaza niba agifite umwanya wo kubinsobanurira uyu munsi. Nabihuza nte?
Wandika uti: "Madamu, niba mugifite umwanya uyu munsi, mwansobanurira neza akazi mwampaye?" Mugifite na mwansobanurira byombi bihuye n'uburyo bwubashye wakoresheje ubwira umuyobozi wawe.
