messages.content: I told a friend, "Nari going to the bus station, imvura itangira kugwa." How can I say the whole event naturally?
Say: "Nari ngiye kuri gare, imvura itangira kugwa." Nari ngiye naturally expresses "I was heading," so the English word going is unnecessary.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0032-5501fa47.

messages.content: How would I tell the same event politely to a driver I do not know?
You can say: "Mwiriwe. Nari ngiye kuri gare, imvura itangira kugwa." The respectful greeting changes the register, while the event itself remains unchanged.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0032-5501fa47.

messages.content: Nari nzi ko "umugani" buri gihe ari igisakuzo. Ni byo?

Oya. Bitewe n'imiterere y'interuro, "umugani" ushobora kuba inkuru yigisha cyangwa interuro ngufi irimo inyigisho. "Igisakuzo" cyo ni ikibazo gihishuye igisubizo kigomba gushakwa. Urugero: "Umwarimu yavuze umugani uvuga ku mwana wakundaga gushushanya." Ariko wavuga uti: "Yabajije igisakuzo, abana bashaka igisubizo."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=vocabulary-definition-usage. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-vocabulary-definition-usage-0033-6c6b6205.

messages.content: Ndashaka kubwira inshuti nti: "Nakunze inkuru yawe. Wanyereka igitabo cyawe?" Nabivuga nte mu buryo bwiyubashye mbwira umwanditsi?
Wavuga uti: "Nakunze inkuru yanyu. Mwanyereka igitabo cyanyu?" Yanyu ijyana n'inkuru, naho cyanyu ikajyana n'igitabo.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0033-236d0829.

messages.content: Reka ngerageze: "Mwiriwe. Nakunze igitabo yanyu. Mwambwira uko mwatangiye kukandika?"
Kosora uti: "Mwiriwe. Nakunze igitabo cyanyu. Mwambwira uko mwatangiye kucyandika?" Hindura yanyu ibe cyanyu, kandi kukandika ibe kucyandika, kuko byombi byerekeza ku gitabo.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0033-236d0829.

messages.content: Mwiriwe. Nakunze igitabo cyanyu. Mwambwira uko mwatangiye kucyandika?
Ni byiza. Ubu interuro yawe iratunganye kandi irubashye. Umwanditsi ashobora gusubiza ati: "Murakoze kubaza. Nshobora kubabwira uko natangiye kucyandika."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0033-236d0829.

messages.content: Please make one Kinyarwanda present-tense sentence using exactly "ibumoso" and "gare," and then give its English meaning.

"Uhindukira ibumoso ugeze kuri gare, hanyuma ugakomeza ugana ku isoko." It means: "You turn left when you reach the bus station, then continue toward the market." The sentence uses exactly "ibumoso" and "gare," and gives directions in the present tense.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=sentence-generation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-sentence-generation-0033-0084f4b4.

messages.content: Can you revise it so it also includes the exact word "ubu" and ends with a question mark?

"Ese ubu uhindukira ibumoso kuri gare, ugakomeza ugana ku isoko?" It means: "Do you now turn left at the bus station and continue toward the market?" It includes exactly "ubu," "ibumoso," and "gare," and ends with "?".

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=sentence-generation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-sentence-generation-0033-0084f4b4.

messages.content: Ndashaka kuvuga harvest mu Kinyarwanda. Nabivuga nte?
Harvest ishobora kuvuga igikorwa cyangwa ibyamaze kuboneka. Urashaka kuvuga igikorwa cyo gusarura, cyangwa umusaruro wabonetse? Tanga interuro kugira ngo mpitemo ijambo rikwiye.

messages.content: Interuro ni iyi: We will harvest beans tomorrow. Ndashaka kuvuga igikorwa cyo gusarura.
Wavuga uti, Ejo tuzasarura ibishyimbo. Aha gusarura ni igikorwa, kandi tuzasarura yerekana ko ari twe tuzabikora ejo. Ntibikenewe kongeramo harvest.

messages.content: When revising an appointment, what is the difference between guhindura gahunda and kwimura inama? Please contrast them in examples.

Guhindura gahunda means changing some part of a plan; kwimura inama specifically means moving a meeting to another time. Compare: "Twahinduye gahunda: tuzahurira ku biro aho guhurira kuri cafe." Here the place changes. "Twimuye inama yo ku wa Gatanu, tuyishyira ku wa Gatandatu." Here the meeting moves from Friday to Saturday.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0034-ef4fd6b4.

messages.content: Ndashaka kuvuga ku giciro cy'ishati nti: "Iyi shati ni cheap."
Vuga uti: "Iyi shati irahendutse." Cheap usimbuze irahendutse; iyi na irahendutse byombi bihuje n'ishati.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0034-3c9e23a3.

messages.content: Naho izi nkweto, navuga nti: "Izi nkweto irahendutse"?
Vuga uti: "Izi nkweto zirahendutse." Kubera ko wavuze izi nkweto, ukoresha zirahendutse, si irahendutse.

messages.content: Nshaka kubibaza umucuruzi mu kinyabupfura. Nabivuga nte?
Uti: "Mwambwira niba izi nkweto zihendutse?" Izi nkweto na zihendutse biracyahuza, kandi mwambwira ituma ikibazo kiba icyiyubashye.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0034-3c9e23a3.

messages.content: Why do I say "Ukuguru kwanjye kurananiwe" but "Amaguru yanjye arananiwe" after a walk?

The related words change to match the noun. With singular "ukuguru," use "kwanjye" and "kurananiwe": "Ukuguru kwanjye kurananiwe nyuma yo kugenda." With plural "amaguru," use "yanjye" and "arananiwe": "Amaguru yanjye arananiwe nyuma yo kugenda." The meaning stays the same apart from one leg versus multiple legs.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0035-9b8bf946.

messages.content: Give me a short Kinyarwanda passage about an everyday visit to a public office. Ask what time the person arrived and why, but do not state how the person traveled.

Passage: Ku wa Kabiri, Keza yageze ku biro by'umurenge saa tatu za mu gitondo. Yari agiye gusaba icyemezo cy'uko atuye. Umukozi yamwakiriye, maze Keza arategereza.

Questions: Keza yageze ku biro ryari? Kuki yari yagiye ku biro?

Answers: Yagezeyo ku wa Kabiri saa tatu za mu gitondo, at 9:00 a.m. Yari agiye gusaba icyemezo cy'uko atuye. The passage does not say how Keza traveled, so that detail cannot be answered from the text.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0035-6110121b.

messages.content: Read this short passage: "Umuhinzi yabanje gutera imbuto, maze arazuhira. Hanyuma asubira mu rugo." What does maze mean here, and what happened second?

Here maze means "then" or "after that" and connects the first action to the next one. The second action was watering the seeds: "Maze arazuhira." Hanyuma is a nearby sequencing word, and here it introduces the final action, returning home.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=vocabulary-definition-usage. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-vocabulary-definition-usage-0036-19d4a30f.

messages.content:
Soma iki gice: Keza yashakaga guhura na Samuel. Samuel yamuhaye amahitamo abiri: ku wa Kabiri saa tatu za mu gitondo cyangwa ku wa Gatatu saa cyenda z'amanywa. Keza yavuze ati: "Njye nzaza ku wa Gatatu saa cyenda z'amanywa, kuko ku wa Kabiri nzaba ndi ku kazi." Samuel aramusubiza ati: "Nanjye icyo gihe kirankwiriye."

Keza yahisemo ryari, kandi "nanjye" ivuga nde?
Keza yahisemo ku wa Gatatu saa cyenda z'amanywa. "Nanjye" ivuga Samuel, kuko ari we wasubizaga Keza. "Njye" yo ivuga Keza.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0036-345b739d.

messages.content: Bazahurira he? Ese ni ku kazi ka Keza?
Aho bazahurira ntihavugwa. Igice kivuga gusa igihe bahisemo; ntidushobora kwemeza ko bazahurira ku kazi ka Keza.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0036-345b739d.

messages.content: Uyu munsi hano hari ibicu kandi harakonje. Nabivuga nte mu kiganiro gisanzwe?
Vuga uti: "Uyu munsi hari ibicu kandi harakonje." Ibyo birumvikana kandi bifite injyana isanzwe. Uzakora iki nimugoroba?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0037-d1a5971c.

messages.content: Nashaka kugenda urugendo nimugoroba.
Byumvikana, ariko hano wavuga neza uti: "Nashaka gutembera nimugoroba." Ni bwo buryo busanzwe bwo kuvuga ko ushaka kujya gutembera.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0037-d1a5971c.

messages.content: Niba imvura iguye, nzaguma mu rugo nsome igitabo.
Ni interuro nziza. Mu kiganiro kigufi wavuga uti: "Niba imvura iguye, nzaguma mu rugo nsome igitabo. Niba itaguye, nzatembera." Ibyo bihuza neza ikirere na gahunda yawe.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0037-d1a5971c.

messages.content: Wanyigisha gukoresha “wowe” na “mwebwe” mu kiganiro kijyanye no kubungabunga ibidukikije?

Ku muntu umwe musanzwe mumenyeranye, wavuga uti: “Wowe urimo gutera igiti hafi y'umugezi.” Mu kubaha umuntu umwe, wavuga uti: “Mwebwe murimo gutera igiti hafi y'umugezi.” Igikorwa n'igiti ntibihinduka; “urimo” ihinduka “murimo” kugira ngo ihuze na “mwebwe.” Akenshi izo nsimburazina zikoreshwa iyo ushaka gushimangira uwo ubwira.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0037-44cdbe9b.

messages.content: Mu nama yo kubungabunga ibidukikije, hari uvuga ati: “Mwebwe muri project lead.” Nakwirinda nte kuvanga indimi bitari ngombwa?

Wavuga uti: “Mwebwe muri umuyobozi w'igikorwa cyo kubungabunga ibidukikije.” Ibyo birumvikana neza mu Kinyarwanda. Ijambo ry'Icyongereza ryakoreshwa gusa niba ari izina ryihariye itsinda ryemeranyijeho; bitabaye ibyo, “umuyobozi w'igikorwa” rirahagije.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0037-44cdbe9b.

messages.content: Nashakaga gusaba inshuti yanjye ko ingeza kwa mama. Nanditse nti: “Nyabuneka, mpa imodoka yawe ingeze kwa mama.” Iyi nteruro irakwiye?

Wavuga uti: “Nyabuneka, wamfasha ukangeza kwa mama mu modoka yawe?” Imvugo “mpa imodoka yawe” yumvikanisha ko ushaka ko aguha cyangwa akagutiza imodoka. “Ukangeza” yo igaragaza neza ko ushaka ko akujyana, kandi “mu modoka yawe” igaragaza uburyo muzagenda.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0038-5346b51e.

messages.content: Ndabyumvise. None se niba nshaka ko atwara njye na mushiki wanjye, nabivuga nte ntahinduye icyo nshaka kumubwira?
Wavuga uti: “Nyabuneka, wadufasha ukatugeza kwa mama mu modoka yawe?” “Du” na “tu” byerekeza kuri wowe na mushiki wawe. Interuro iracyamusaba kubatwara; ntimusaba kubatiza imodoka.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0038-5346b51e.

messages.content: Soma uyu mwandiko: "Abaturage bateganya umunsi wo gusukura aho batuye. Bashoboraga guhura ku wa Gatandatu saa tatu za mu gitondo cyangwa ku Cyumweru saa munani. Bahisemo ku wa Gatandatu saa tatu za mu gitondo kuko benshi ari bwo baboneka. Aho bazahurira ntiharamenyekana." Answer in English: Which option did they choose, why did they choose it, and where will they meet? Does "bahisemo" mean "they chose" or "they visited"?

They chose Saturday at saa tatu za mu gitondo, or 9:00 a.m., because most people are available then. The meeting place is not given; the passage says it is not yet known. "Bahisemo" means "they chose," which fits the comparison between the two options.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0038-0024e62b.

messages.content: What is the difference between "Abaturage batera ibiti buri mwaka" and "Abaturage barimo gutera ibiti ubu"? Also, what changes if I replace "abaturage" with "umuturage"?

"Abaturage batera ibiti buri mwaka" describes a repeated present habit: "Residents plant trees every year." "Abaturage barimo gutera ibiti ubu" presents the action as underway now: "Residents are planting trees now."

With one resident, the agreement changes: "Umuturage atera ibiti buri mwaka" and "Umuturage arimo gutera ibiti ubu." Thus, "batera/barimo" matches plural "abaturage," while "atera/arimo" matches singular "umuturage."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0039-517c7b2d.

messages.content: I am practicing questions about a story. We know that Aline finished a mural on Friday, but the story does not say where. How do I ask when and where she finished it?

You can ask: “Aline yarangije igishushanyo cyo ku rukuta ryari?” for “When did Aline finish the mural?” For the location, ask: “Aline yakirangije he?” The marker “ki” in yakirangije refers to igishushanyo.

messages.content: What answers can I give to those two questions from the information we have?
For the first question: “Yakirangije ku wa Gatanu.” For the second, we cannot give a location because none was provided. You can say: “Aho yakirangirije ntihavuzwe.”

messages.content: Is “Aline yakirangije he ku wa Gatanu?” a correct question? Could I answer “Mu nzu ye”?
Yes, “Aline yakirangije he ku wa Gatanu?” is correct; it asks for the unknown location while retaining Friday. You cannot answer “Mu nzu ye,” because neither a house nor its ownership was mentioned.

messages.content: My draft is "Ejo I have a meeting na Aline saa munani." How do I naturally say, "I will meet Aline again tomorrow at exactly 2:00 p.m."?
Say: "Nzongera guhura na Aline ejo saa munani zuzuye z'amanywa." Here, "nzongera guhura" preserves "will meet again," while "saa munani zuzuye z'amanywa" gives the exact time, 2:00 p.m.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0040-d9e18702.

messages.content: The time changed. How do I say, "We moved that appointment to exactly 3:00 p.m., but I will still meet Aline again tomorrow"?
Say: "Twimuye iyo gahunda tuyishyira ejo saa cyenda zuzuye z'amanywa, ariko ejo nzongera guhura na Aline." This keeps the revised time, 3:00 p.m., and "nzongera" still carries the meaning of "again."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0040-d9e18702.

messages.content: Ejo nagiye gutembera kuva kuri hoteli kugeza ku isoko.
Mu buryo busanzwe wavuga uti: "Ejo navuye kuri hoteli njya ku isoko n'amaguru." Ibi byerekana neza aho watangiriye n'aho wari ugiye.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0040-8e0c7922.

messages.content: Ngeze ku isoko, nabajije umugore aho isomero iri.
Ni byiza. Kosora gusa uti: "Ngeze ku isoko, nabajije umugore aho isomero riri." Ijambo "riri" rihura na "isomero."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0040-8e0c7922.

messages.content: Yambwiye ko isomero riri hagati ya banki na pariki. Nanyuze hafi ya pariki.
Ibyo birumvikana neza. "Hagati ya banki na pariki" herekana aho isomero riri, naho "hafi ya pariki" herekana aho wanyuze. Wahageze ryari?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0040-8e0c7922.

messages.content: Nahageze saa kumi, ariko isomero ryari rifunze, nuko nsubira kuri hoteli.
Birumvikana. "Nahageze" irasubira ku isomero mwari mumaze kuvuga, kandi "nsubira kuri hoteli" igaragaza neza aho wasubiye.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0040-8e0c7922.

messages.content: Translate this into natural English and explain how we know “inama” is plural: “Mwansobanurira niba inama zombi zasubitswe kugeza ku wa Gatanu utaha?”

“Could you clarify whether both meetings were postponed until next Friday?”

“Inama” can refer to one meeting or several without changing its visible form. Here, “zombi” means “both,” and the “za-” in “zasubitswe” agrees with multiple meetings, so the sentence is clearly plural.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0041-7910c90f.

messages.content: I want to say, "I go to school on foot." Is "Njya ku ishuri na amaguru" correct?

Almost. Say: "Njya ku ishuri n'amaguru." Your words "Njya ku ishuri" were already correct. Before the vowel at the beginning of "amaguru," write "na" as "n'." The expression "n'amaguru" naturally means "on foot."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0041-5771011c.

messages.content:
Soma iki gice: "Mu isomero ry'ishuri harimo ibitabo bitatu bishya n'ikaye imwe nshya. Sara ahitamo ibitabo bibiri bishya byo gusoma nimugoroba."

What does Sara choose, and what color are the items she chooses?
Sara chooses two new books: "ibitabo bibiri bishya." Both "bibiri" and "bishya" match the plural noun "ibitabo." The passage does not say what color the books are, so we cannot supply a color.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0041-2e4e3095.

messages.content: Nashakaga kuvuga ibizaba ejo. Interuro yanjye ngo "Ejo naruhuka kare kugira ngo ngire imbaraga" irakwiye?
Kosora uti: "Ejo nzaruhuka kare kugira ngo ngire imbaraga." Hindura gusa "naruhuka" ibe "nzaruhuka," kuko "nza-" ari yo igaragaza ko kuruhuka bizaba ejo. Ibindi wari wabivuze neza.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0042-c80b7b97.

messages.content: Give me a short Kinyarwanda passage about a relaxing walk. Include the exact time and number of trees, but do not say who accompanied the person. Then ask me three comprehension questions in English.

“Ku wa Gatandatu saa moya za mu gitondo, Keza yagiye gutembera mu busitani. Yabonye ibiti bitatu n'indabo nyinshi. Nyuma y'iminota makumyabiri, yasubiye mu rugo aruhutse.”

Questions: What time did Keza go for the walk? How many trees did she see? Who went with her?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0042-ac5bcaad.

messages.content: Keza yagiye saa moya za mu gitondo kandi yabonye ibiti bitatu. I think she went with her sister.

Your first two answers are supported: “Yagiye saa moya za mu gitondo” and “Yabonye ibiti bitatu.” The passage does not mention a sister or any companion, so we cannot say who went with Keza. Notice the plural pair: igiti becomes ibiti, and “three trees” is ibiti bitatu.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0042-ac5bcaad.

messages.content: I tried to say, “She told me that I am well today”: “Yambwiye ko umeze neza uyu munsi.” Is that correct?
The corrected sentence is: “Yambwiye ko meze neza uyu munsi.” Keep yambwiye because it correctly means “she told me.” Change umeze to meze because the person who is well is “I,” not “you.”

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0043-aeb99c2e.

messages.content: If I mean, “She told you that she is well today,” can I say: “Yambwiye ko ameze neza uyu munsi”?
Say: “Yakubwiye ko ameze neza uyu munsi.” Change yambwiye to yakubwiye because the person being told is now “you.” “Ameze” is already correct because it refers to “she.”

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0043-aeb99c2e.

messages.content:
Read this passage: Mutesi asks her friend, 'Uraza saa cyenda z'amanywa?' Later, she asks the director, 'Muraza saa cyenda z'amanywa?' Both accept that time. The meeting place is not mentioned.

What time do they choose, which question is formal, and where will they meet?

They choose saa cyenda z'amanywa, or 3:00 p.m. The formal question is 'Muraza saa cyenda z'amanywa?' because Mutesi is addressing the director respectfully. The passage does not say where they will meet.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0043-11be7cd4.

messages.content: In a chat, what does "kuvuga ikintu mu yandi magambo" mean? Please give me a short example.

It means "to say something in different words," or to paraphrase. Urugero: "Yavuze ubutumwa mu yandi magambo kugira ngo busobanuke." This means that he or she restated the message to make it clear, rather than repeating the exact same words.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=vocabulary-definition-usage. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-vocabulary-definition-usage-0044-167a036e.

messages.content: Ejo nasuye inshuti yanjye mu mudugudu mushya.
Ni byiza. "Nasuye" yerekana igikorwa cyabaye ejo. Wabonye iki muri uwo mudugudu?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0044-e3c53bc2.

messages.content: None navuga nti: "Ejo nasuye inshuti yanjye, nabonye amazu meza, ariko sinamenye igihe ayo mazu yubatswe."
Yego, iyo nteruro irumvikana neza. "Nasuye," "nabonye," na "sinamenye" byose bivuga ibyabaye mu gihe cyashize, kandi ntiwongeyemo igihe utazi.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0044-e3c53bc2.

messages.content: How do I say, 'I feel tired today, so I will rest now and revise tomorrow's plan' in Kinyarwanda? Please explain one spelling choice.

Uyu munsi ndumva naniwe, ni yo mpamvu ngiye kuruhuka ubu, kandi ejo nzavugurura gahunda y'ejo.

'Ndumva naniwe' is a natural way to describe feeling tired. In 'y'ejo,' the apostrophe joins the linking form 'ya' to 'ejo,' giving the meaning 'tomorrow's.'

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0045-77a372a3.

messages.content: Is this summary correct: "Ejo mu gitondo, umwana babiri babyutse saa moya hanyuma bajya ku ishuri"?

Almost. Say: "Ejo mu gitondo, abana babiri babyutse saa moya hanyuma bajya ku ishuri." Change only "umwana" to the plural "abana." The words "babiri," "babyutse," and "bajya" already refer to more than one child.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0045-a0a11967.

messages.content: Soma iki gice: "Ku wa Mbere saa moya za mu gitondo, Keza yatangiye gahunda yo kugera ku ntego ze. Yanditse intego eshatu: gusoma ibitabo bibiri buri kwezi, gukora imyitozo iminsi itatu mu cyumweru, no kuzigama amafaranga make buri wa Gatanu. Nimugoroba, yasuzumye ibyo yari yakoze." Vuga muri make ibyabaye, igihe Keza yatangiriye, umubare w'intego, n'uwamufashije.

Keza yatangiye gahunda ye ku wa Mbere saa moya za mu gitondo. Yanditse intego eshatu, maze nimugoroba asuzuma ibyo yari yakoze. Uwafashije Keza ntavugwa muri icyo gice. Mu bwinshi, "igitabo" kiba "ibitabo," kandi tuvuga "ibitabo bibiri."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0045-a5f86f94.

messages.content: Mu gutegura gahunda y'umunsi, "kubanza" na "hanyuma" bitandukaniye he, kandi mbishyira he mu nteruro?

"Kubanza" yerekana igikorwa ukora mbere y'ikindi, kandi ikurikirwa n'igikorwa: "Nzabanza gukora isuku, hanyuma njye ku isoko." "Hanyuma" itangiza igikorwa gikurikiraho. Uhinduye gahunda wavuga uti: "Nzabanza kujya ku isoko, hanyuma ngaruke nkore isuku." Naho "mbere yo" igereranya ibihe: "Mbere yo kujya ku isoko, nzakora isuku."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=vocabulary-definition-usage. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-vocabulary-definition-usage-0046-b261ee24.

messages.content: Ejo nagiye kuri gare gufata bisi ijya i Musanze.
Ni interuro nziza. Wategereje bisi he?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0046-934c1596.

messages.content: Nategereje mu cyapa hafi ya gare.
Vuga uti: "Nategereje ku cyapa hafi ya gare." Dukoresha "ku cyapa" aho gukoresha "mu cyapa." Bisi igeze, wicaye he?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0046-934c1596.

messages.content: Navuyemo ku isoko, hanyuma njya mu iduka.
Wavuga neza uti: "Navuyemo ngeze ku isoko, hanyuma njya mu iduka." "Ku isoko" ni ho waviriye muri bisi, naho "mu iduka" ni ho wahise ujya.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0046-934c1596.

messages.content: Mu gikorwa cy'abaturage, nabwira umuturanyi nti: 'Wamfasha kuri iyi event?' Ndashaka kubivuga mu Kinyarwanda kandi mu buryo bwiyubashye.

Wavuga uti: 'Wamfasha muri iki gikorwa?' Ijambo 'event' risimburwa na 'igikorwa,' kandi 'iki' kijyana na 'igikorwa.' Niba ubwira umuntu ushaka guha icyubahiro, wavuga uti: 'Mwamfasha muri iki gikorwa?'

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=register-culture-code-switching. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-register-culture-code-switching-0046-b400c993.

messages.content: How do I ask whether someone cooked beans, as opposed to asking what they cooked?

To ask for a yes-or-no answer, say: "Ese watetse ibishyimbo?" This means "Did you cook beans?" To ask which food the person cooked, say: "Watetse iki?" This means "What did you cook?" In the second question, "iki" replaces the food that is not yet known.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0047-0dafed41.

messages.content: Mfite intego yo kunoza Igifaransa. Ndagereranya kwiga iminota mirongo itatu buri mugoroba no kwiga amasaha atatu ku wa Gatandatu. Aha nakoresha "intego," "umugambi" cyangwa "gahunda"?

Kunoza Igifaransa ni intego, kuko ari icyo ushaka kugeraho. Kwiga buri mugoroba cyangwa ku wa Gatandatu ni gahunda, kuko bigena uko n'igihe uziga. Umugambi ni icyo wiyemeje gushyira mu bikorwa, ariko "gahunda" ni ryo jambo risobanutse kurushaho hano.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0047-fb0dd065.

messages.content: Nahisemo uburyo bwa mbere kuko nshaka gukomeza kwiga buri munsi. Ese "gukomeza" no "guhoraho" bisobanura kimwe?

Si kimwe neza. "Gukomeza kwiga" ni kutareka kwiga; "guhoraho" ni ukumara igihe kirekire cyangwa kutarangira. Ku cyemezo cyawe, birasanzwe kuvuga uti: "Nziga iminota mirongo itatu buri mugoroba kugira ngo nkomeze kwiga buri munsi."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0047-fb0dd065.

messages.content: None nandike nti: "Nahisemo uburyo bwa mbere kuko buroroshye gukurikiza kurusha ubwa kabiri"?

Igitekerezo cyawe kirumvikana. Interuro isobanutse kurushaho ni iyi: "Nahisemo uburyo bwa mbere kuko ari bwo bworoshye gukurikiza buri munsi kurusha uburyo bwa kabiri." Gusubiramo "uburyo bwa kabiri" bituma ikigereranywa kititiranywa n'ikindi kintu.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0047-fb0dd065.

messages.content: Interuro yanjye ni iyi: "Mbanza kuruhuka, hanyuma mbere nkanywa amazi." Kuki yumvikana nabi?
Wayikosora uti: "Mbanza kuruhuka, hanyuma nkanywa amazi." "Mbanza" yamaze kwerekana igikorwa cya mbere, naho "hanyuma" ikerekana igikurikiraho. Ni yo mpamvu "mbere" idakenewe nyuma ya "hanyuma."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0048-d15a1b3c.

messages.content: Nyuma yabyo nshaka kuvuga nti: "Nyuma y'ibyo, nuko njya gutembera." Ibyo byo birakwiye?
Vuga uti: "Nyuma y'ibyo, njya gutembera." Ushobora no kuvuga uti: "Hanyuma njya gutembera." "Nuko" ntikenewe hano, kuko "nyuma y'ibyo" yamaze kwerekana ikurikirana ry'ibikorwa.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0048-d15a1b3c.

messages.content:
Soma uyu mwandiko: Ku wa Gatatu, Aline yageze kuri gare saa moya n'igice za mu gitondo. Yaguze itike y'imodoka ijya i Musanze. Imodoka yagombaga guhaguruka saa mbili za mu gitondo.

Aline yageze kuri gare ryari? Imodoka yajyaga he kandi yagombaga guhaguruka ryari? Igiciro cy'itike cyari angahe? Nanone, 'itike' ni iki?

Aline yageze kuri gare saa moya n'igice za mu gitondo. Imodoka yajyaga i Musanze kandi yagombaga guhaguruka saa mbili za mu gitondo. Igiciro cy'itike ntikivugwa mu mwandiko. Itike ni icyemezo kigaragaza ko umuntu yemerewe gukora urugendo.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0048-76ebee5f.

messages.content: In the sentence "Abahinzi bazasarura ibigori mu kwezi gutaha," what does "bazasarura" mean, and on what exact date will they harvest?

"Bazasarura" means "they will harvest." The sentence places the harvest in the future: "Abahinzi bazasarura ibigori mu kwezi gutaha." It contrasts with "bazatera ibigori," meaning "they will plant maize." The exact harvest date is not given; the sentence says only "next month."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=vocabulary-definition-usage. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-vocabulary-definition-usage-0049-2ef974a4.

messages.content: Buri gitondo ntegereza bisi aho zihagarara. Iyo ije, ndayinjiramo. Ese "-yi-" muri "ndayinjiramo" isimbura iki?
"-yi-" isimbura "bisi": winjira muri bisi. Interuro yawe irumvikana neza kandi ikomeza kuvuga urugendo rwa buri gitondo.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0049-0f7ae725.

messages.content: Nyuma mbona intebe irimo ubusa, ndayicaraho kugeza bisi igeze aho nkorera. Aha "-yi-" iracyasimbura bisi?
Oya. Muri "ndayicaraho," "-yi-" isimbura "intebe," si "bisi," kuko ari intebe wicaraho. Muri "bisi igeze aho nkorera," wongeye kuvuga izina "bisi" ku buryo icyo ushaka kuvuga gisobanutse.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0049-0f7ae725.

messages.content: Translate this into natural English: 'Mbabarira, mwambwira niba iyi bisi ijya i Huye, kandi niba ihaguruka mbere ya saa yine za mu gitondo?' Also, what makes the request polite?

Excuse me, could you tell me whether this bus goes to Huye and whether it leaves before 10:00 a.m.?

'Mbabarira' politely gets the listener's attention, while 'mwambwira' is naturally rendered as 'could you tell me' rather than the more direct 'tell me.' The translation preserves both questions: the destination and the departure time.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0050-bd47099f.

messages.content: Ese iyi nteruro irakwiye: "Inama uzatangira saa tatu izabera ku biro by'umurenge"?

Hindura "uzatangira" uvuge "izatangira": "Inama izatangira saa tatu izabera ku biro by'umurenge." Inshinga "izatangira" yerekeza ku nama, naho "uzatangira" yakwerekeza ku muntu ubwirwa. Igice "izabera ku biro by'umurenge" cyari gisanzwe gikwiriye.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0050-fba3366c.

messages.content: Soma aka gace: "Ku wa Gatandatu saa kumi z'umugoroba, Aline yari ananiwe nyuma yo kwiga. Yafashe akaruhuko, anywa amazi, maze mushiki we amuzanira igitabo. Aline aramubwira ati: 'Ndakigusubiza ejo.'" At what time was Aline tired, and what did her sister bring her?
Aline yari ananiwe saa kumi z'umugoroba, ni ukuvuga 4:00 p.m. Mushiki we yamuzaniye igitabo. Those are the only requested details stated in the passage.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0050-d01bb0b2.

messages.content: Why does "Mushiki we amuzanira Aline igitabo" sound unnatural when I retell the passage?

Mu- in amuzanira already represents Aline, so repeating Aline is unnecessary in a neutral sentence. Say either "Mushiki we amuzanira igitabo" or, with the name stated, "Mushiki we azanira Aline igitabo."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0050-d01bb0b2.

messages.content: Mu kuvuga imirimo yo mu murima, "hanyuma" risobanura iki? Ritandukaniye he na "nyuma yo"?

"Hanyuma" means "then" or "after that" and links two actions: "Twabanje guhinga, hanyuma dutera imbuto." "Nyuma yo" means "after" and is followed by an action or event: "Nyuma yo gutera imbuto, twavomerera umurima." So, use "hanyuma" to continue a sequence and "nyuma yo" to introduce what happened before the next action.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=vocabulary-definition-usage. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-vocabulary-definition-usage-0051-1f729b37.

messages.content: How do I say, "I do not draw; I tell stories"?
Say: "Sinshushanya; mbara inkuru." "Sinshushanya" is the negative idea, while "mbara inkuru" states what you do instead. Ni izihe nkuru ubara?

messages.content: Read this passage: “Ku wa Kane, Keza yagombaga kuganira n'umwarimu we. Umwarimu yamuhaye amahitamo abiri: saa tatu za mu gitondo cyangwa saa munani z'amanywa. Keza yahisemo saa munani kuko saa tatu yari kuba ari mu isomo. Bemeranyije guhurira ku ishuri, ariko ntibavuze icyumba.” Which time did Keza choose, and why?
Keza yahisemo saa munani z'amanywa, or 2:00 p.m. She rejected saa tatu za mu gitondo because she would be in class at that time.

messages.content: What room number did they choose? Also, what does “yari kuba ari mu isomo” mean in this context?
Nta nimero y'icyumba ivugwa muri uwo mwandiko. In context, “yari kuba ari mu isomo” means “she would be in class”; it explains why the morning option did not suit her.

messages.content: Mu mupira w'amaguru, amagambo "gutsinda" no "kunyagira" atandukaniye he? Mpa ingero zoroshye.

"Gutsinda" ni ukubona amanota cyangwa ibitego byinshi kurusha uwo mukina. "Kunyagira" ni gutsinda ku buryo burushijeho kugaragara, akenshi harimo ikinyuranyo kinini cy'ibitego. Urugero: "Ikipe yacu yatsinze indi ibitego bibiri kuri kimwe." Ariko wavuga uti: "Ikipe yacu yanyagiye indi ibitego bitanu ku busa." Buri kunyagira ni ugutsinda, ariko gutsinda kose si ukunyagira.

messages.content: Ku wa Gatandatu, Keza yasuye mukuru we Amina. Eric yashatse kubasanga, ariko ntiyaje kuko yari ku kazi. Mfasha gukora incamake irimo impakanyi.
Ushobora kuvuga uti: "Keza yasuye Amina, ariko Eric ntiyaje." In English: "Keza visited Amina, but Eric did not come." Impakanyi "ntiyaje" yerekeza kuri Eric.

Oya. Iyo nteruro ihindura amakuru yombi. Incamake ihuye n'inkuru ni: "Keza yasuye Amina, ariko Eric ntiyaje." Keza yarasuye Amina; Eric ni we utaraje.

Muri iyo nteruro, "we" ryerekeza kuri Keza: Amina ni mukuru wa Keza. Final summary: "On Saturday, Keza visited her older sister Amina, but Eric did not come because he was at work."

messages.content: Kuki tuvuga ngo “Umutsima urashyushye,” ariko tukavuga ngo “Ibirayi birashyushye”?

Ijambo rivuga uko ibiryo bimeze rihinduka rigahuza n'izina. Tuvuga ngo “Umutsima urashyushye,” ariko tukavuga ngo “Ibirayi birashyushye.” Bityo, “ura-” ikoreshwa hamwe n'umutsima, naho “bira-” igakoreshwa hamwe n'ibirayi.

messages.content: Andikira agace kagufi kavuga ku guhamagara kuri telefone mu gihe kizaza. Hanyuma umbwire igihe uwo muntu azahamagara n'aho azaba ari. Niba ahantu hatavuzwe, ntuhahimbe.
Agace: "Ejo saa moya z'umugoroba, Aline azahamagara musaza we kuri telefone. Bazaganira ku byo bakoze uwo munsi." Igihe ni ejo saa moya z'umugoroba. Agace ntikavuga aho Aline azaba ari.

messages.content: Nabaza nte umukozi wo ku murenge aho ibiro by'irangamimerere biherereye? Kandi nabibaza nte inshuti yanjye?

Ku mukozi, vuga mu buryo bw'icyubahiro uti: "Mwambwira aho ibiro by'irangamimerere biherereye, nyabuneka?" Ku nshuti imwe, wavuga uti: "Wambwira aho ibiro by'irangamimerere biherereye?" "Mwambwira" irubashye, naho "wambwira" ikoreshwa mu mvugo isanzwe ku muntu umwe musanzwe mumenyeranye.

messages.content: Soma aka gace: "Saa tatu za mu gitondo, Mutesi yafunguye application yamufashije kohereza ubutumwa kuri Kalisa. Ubutumwa bwoherejwe bwari bufite ifoto. Aka gace ntikavuga uwigishije Mutesi gukoresha iyo gahunda." Simbuza ijambo ritari Ikinyarwanda, hanyuma usubize: Ni ryari Mutesi yafunguye iyo gahunda? Yohereje iki? Ni nde wamwigishije kuyikoresha?

Ijambo "application" risimbuzwa "porogaramu": "Mutesi yafunguye porogaramu yamufashije kohereza ubutumwa kuri Kalisa." Yayifunguye saa tatu za mu gitondo. Yohereje ubutumwa bwari bufite ifoto. Uwamwigishije gukoresha porogaramu ntavugwa muri aka gace.

messages.content: Why do people say "Bisi irahaguruka kare" but "Bisi zirahaguruka kare"?

The first sentence means one bus leaves early: "Bisi irahaguruka kare." The subject marker is "i-." The second means multiple buses leave early: "Bisi zirahaguruka kare." For plural buses, the marker changes to "zi-." Mu nteruro zombi, bisi ni zo zihaguruka.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0057-2925f9eb.

messages.content: Soma aka gace: "Mariya yari yateganyije kujya ku biro by'umurenge wacu ku wa Kabiri saa yine za mu gitondo gufata icyangombwa cye. Nyuma yahinduye gahunda ye kuko musaza we yari akeneye igare ryabo. Yemeje kujyayo ku wa Gatatu saa munani z'amanywa akoresheje bisi." What was her original plan, what is the revised plan, whose bicycle is mentioned, and what is the employee's name?

Gahunda ya mbere yari ukujya ku biro by'umurenge ku wa Kabiri saa yine za mu gitondo, ni ukuvuga 10:00 a.m. Gahunda nshya ni ukujyayo ku wa Gatatu saa munani z'amanywa, ni ukuvuga 2:00 p.m., akoresheje bisi. "Igare ryabo" refers to the bicycle shared by Mariya and her brother. The passage does not give an employee's name.

messages.content:
Soma iki gika, hanyuma usubize ibibazo.

Ku wa Gatandatu, Keza yavuye mu rugo saa mbili za mu gitondo. Yabanje kunyura imbere y'iposita, maze akomeza kugenda kugera ku isomero. Hanyuma yafashe bisi igana ku isoko. Ageze ku isoko, yaguze imbuto, nyuma abaza umucuruzi inzira igana kuri gare.

Keza yavuye mu rugo saa ngahe? Ni ibihe bikorwa bibiri yakoze mbere yo kubaza inzira? Ibara rya bisi ryari irihe? Ijambo "igana" risobanura iki aho ryakoreshejwe kuri bisi no ku nzira?

Keza yavuye mu rugo saa mbili za mu gitondo. Mbere yo kubaza inzira, yafashe bisi, hanyuma agura imbuto. Ibara rya bisi ntirivugwa muri iki gika. "Igana" yerekana aho ikintu kerekeza: bisi yerekezaga ku isoko, naho inzira yerekezaga kuri gare.

messages.content: Buri gitondo ndabyuka saa kumi n'ebyiri, hanyuma ngakora shower. Nabivuga nte mu Kinyarwanda gusa?

Vuga uti: "Buri gitondo ndabyuka saa kumi n'ebyiri, hanyuma nkiyuhagira." "Kwiyuhagira" ni ko kuvuga icyo washakaga kuvuga. "Kubyuka" ni kuva mu bitotsi, naho "guhaguruka" akenshi ni kuva aho wari wicaye cyangwa uryamye.

messages.content: None se nshobora kuvuga nti: "Ndabyuka, ariko nkomeza kuryama; nyuma ndahaguruka"?
Yego, birumvikana neza. "Ndabyuka" bivuga ko ibitotsi bishize, naho "ndahaguruka" bivuga ko uva mu buriri. Urugero: "Mbyuka saa kumi n'ebyiri, ariko mpaguruka saa moya."

messages.content: Nateye igiti kimwe ku ishuri. Navuga nte ko icyo giti ari icyanjye?
Uvuga uti: “Iki ni igiti cyanjye.” Iyo ari byinshi, uvuga uti: “Ibi ni ibiti byanjye.” Cy- ikoreshwa kuri igiti, naho by- igakoreshwa kuri ibiti.

messages.content: Amababi wacyo aracyari mato. Ni byo?
Hindura wacyo ube yacyo: “Amababi yacyo aracyari mato.” Amababi ni yo agena y-, kandi yacyo yerekana ko ayo mababi ari ay'igiti wavuze.

messages.content: Noneho navuga nti: “Amababi yacyo aracyari mato, ariko igiti cyanjye kirakura.”
Ni byo. “Yacyo” iracyerekeza kuri cya giti kimwe wateye, naho “cyanjye” ikerekana ko igiti ari icyawe.

messages.content: Mu nteruro "Twubatse ikigega cy'amazi," why do we write "cy'amazi" instead of "cya amazi"? Mpa n'izindi ngero zijyanye no kubungabunga ibidukikije.

Iyo "cya" cyangwa "ya" ikurikiwe n'ijambo ritangirwa n'inyajwi, inyajwi ya nyuma irakurwaho: "Twubatse ikigega cy'amazi." Ariko iguma imbere y'ingombajwi: "Twubatse ikigega cya peteroli." Dore irindi tandukaniro: "Twakusanyije imyanda y'amababi," ariko "Twakusanyije imyanda ya pulasitiki." The agreement also changes with the noun: "ikigega" takes "cya," while "imyanda" takes "ya."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0060-6e36a589.

messages.content: Please translate: "The new employees attend two meetings every Monday." I tried: "Abakozi nshya bitabira inama bibiri buri wa Mbere." Why does it sound unnatural?

A natural translation is: "Abakozi bashya bitabira inama ebyiri buri wa Mbere." The plural noun "abakozi" requires "bashya," and plural "inama" takes "ebyiri." That is why "nshya" and "bibiri" do not agree correctly here.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0061-c668f80c.

messages.content: Soma ibi: "Keza atuye mu mudugudu uri hafi y'isoko. Buri gitondo agenda n'amaguru ajya ku ishuri." Ku kibazo ngo "Keza atuye he?" nasubije nti: "Keza atuye hafi ku isoko." Ese ni byo?

Igisubizo kiboneye ni: "Keza atuye hafi y'isoko." Hindura gusa "hafi ku isoko" ukavugamo "hafi y'isoko," kuko ari ko havugwa ko ahantu hegeranye n'ahandi. Ibindi wari wavuze bihuye n'ibiri mu mwandiko.

messages.content: Soma aka gace: “Ku wa Gatandatu, Mutesi yabwiye Shema ati: ‘Fata ikaye yanjye iri ku ntebe, uyishyire mu isakoshi yawe, hanyuma uzane igitabo cyawe mu cyumba cy'imyitozo saa cyenda.’” Ikaye ni iya nde? Shema agomba kuyishyira he? Igitabo ni icya nde? Azakizana ryari, kandi icyumba gifite nimero ingahe?
Ikaye ni iya Mutesi. Shema agomba kuyishyira mu isakoshi ye. Igitabo ni icya Shema, kandi azakizana saa cyenda. Nimero y'icyumba ntiyavuzwe muri ako gace.

messages.content: I want to say, “The employee who works at reception helps visitors.” I wrote: “Umukozi ikora ku biro bishinzwe kwakira abantu afasha abashyitsi.” Is it correct?

Almost. The corrected sentence is: “Umukozi ukora ku biro bishinzwe kwakira abantu afasha abashyitsi.” Only “ikora” changes to “ukora” because the relative clause describes “umukozi,” a person. The rest of your sentence already preserves the intended meaning.

messages.content: Translate into Kinyarwanda: "The two new balls are small." Please explain one important choice.
"Imipira ibiri mishya ni mito." Imipira is plural, so "new" becomes mishya and "small" becomes mito. These forms agree with imipira, while ibiri preserves the exact number, two.

messages.content: Nanditse nti: "Mu rainy season, imvura igwa in Kigali." Nabikosora nte mu Kinyarwanda?
Wavuga uti: "Mu gihe cy'imvura, imvura igwa i Kigali." Rainy season yasimbujwe igihe cy'imvura, naho in Kigali ihinduka i Kigali. Ibindi wari wanditse ni byo.

messages.content: Ese iyi yo imeze ite: "Mu summer tujya ku musozi kubera ko haba hashyushye"?
Ikosorwa ni: "Mu mpeshyi tujya ku musozi kubera ko haba hashyushye." Summer yasimbujwe impeshyi. Ku musozi na kubera ko haba hashyushye byari bisanzwe ari byo, bityo ntibihinduka.

messages.content:
Soma iki gika:

Ku wa Kabiri saa tatu za mu gitondo, abaturage bo mu Mudugudu wa Kabeza bagiye ku biro by'umurenge gusaba serivisi y'amazi. Umukozi yarababwiye ati: "Izi documents muzizane ejo, kandi iyi form muyuzuze neza."

At what time did the residents go? Repair the unnecessary English words while keeping noun agreement correct. How much did the service cost?

They went at saa tatu za mu gitondo, or 9:00 a.m. The repaired instruction is: “Muzane izi nyandiko ejo, kandi mwuzuze neza iyi fishi.” “Izi” agrees with plural “nyandiko,” while “iyi” agrees with singular “fishi.” Nta giciro cya serivisi cyavuzwe muri iki gika, so its cost cannot be determined.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0063-166293b7.

messages.content: Ndimo kwandika inkuru. Imvugo "nyuma yo" nayikoresha nte mvuga ko nabanje kumva inkuru, hanyuma nkayishushanya?

Vuga uti: "Nyuma yo kumva inkuru, narayishushanyije." Ushobora no kuvuga uti: "Nashushanyije inkuru nyuma yo kuyumva." Mu ya mbere, icyo wabanje gukora kiza mbere; mu ya kabiri, icyo wakoze nyuma ni cyo kibanza kuvugwa.

messages.content: Nagerageje nti: "Nyuma yo inkuru kumva, narayishushanyije." Iryo tondeka rirakwiye?
Oya. Vuga uti: "Nyuma yo kumva inkuru, narayishushanyije." Nyuma ya "nyuma yo," ubanza igikorwa "kumva," ugakurizaho icyo wumvise, ari cyo "inkuru."

messages.content: I rent one house near the market. Nshaka kuvuga “my house” mu Kinyarwanda.
Say “inzu yanjye.” A full sentence is: “Inzu yanjye iri hafi y'isoko.” Yanjye shows that the house is yours.

messages.content: I mean their house: inyuma y'inzu ye.
Noneho interuro yuzuye ni: “Ubusitani bwe buri inyuma y'inzu ye.” Bwe yerekeza ku busitani bw'umuturanyi, kandi ye ikerekana ko inzu na yo ari iy'uwo muturanyi.

messages.content: Why does "Nteka ibiryo iryoshye" sound unnatural if I mean "I cook tasty food"?

Say: "Nteka ibiryo biryoshye." Both iryoshye and biryoshye mean "tasty," but the form must match the noun. Ibiryo takes biryoshye. By contrast, a singular noun such as isupu takes iryoshye: "Nteka isupu iryoshye."

messages.content: Ndi ku biro by'umurenge. Ndashaka kubwira umukozi nti: "Ndashaka impapuro yo gusaba icyemezo."
Vuga uti: "Ndashaka impapuro zo gusaba icyemezo." Impapuro ni nyinshi, bityo zijyana na zo, si yo. Nk'umukozi nakubaza nti: "Murashaka gusaba icyemezo ki?"

messages.content: Translate into Kinyarwanda: “If I wake up early tomorrow, I will take a walk before work.” Please briefly explain the conditional.

“Nimbyuka kare ejo, nzatembera mbere yo kujya ku kazi.” Here, “nimbyuka” introduces the condition, while “nzatembera” gives the future result. “Gutembera” is the natural everyday expression for taking a walk.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0066-39230e54.

messages.content: How can I say, "One farmer plants one tree, but the farmers water the trees"? I want to understand the plural changes.

Say: "Umuhinzi atera igiti kimwe, ariko abahinzi bavomera ibiti." Umuhinzi becomes abahinzi, and igiti becomes ibiti. The verbs also agree: atera has one farmer as its subject, while bavomera has several farmers.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0066-f39c4bc9.

messages.content: Why do we not simply add an English-style "s"? Also, how can I show whether inka means one cow or several cows?

Kinyarwanda commonly shows number through noun prefixes and agreement rather than adding "s." With inka, the noun itself can look the same: "Inka imwe iri mu rwuri" means one cow is in the pasture, while "Inka nyinshi ziri mu rwuri" means many cows are in the pasture. Imwe or nyinshi and the change from iri to ziri make the number clear.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0066-f39c4bc9.

messages.content: At work I said, "Ejo nzajya kuri meeting." How can I say that fully in Kinyarwanda, and which part shows the future?
Say: "Ejo nzajya mu nama." It means "Tomorrow I will go to the meeting." In nzajya, the future meaning appears in -za-. Compare: "Ubu ngiye mu nama" means "I am going to the meeting now."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0067-257b3d70.

messages.content: How would I say, "Tomorrow we will go to the meeting and prepare the document"?
Say: "Ejo tuzajya mu nama kandi tuzategura inyandiko." The subject changes from "I" to "we," so nzajya becomes tuzajya. The second future verb also begins with tuza-: tuzategura.

messages.content: Ndashaka gutegura umukino w'umupira w'amaguru ku wa Gatandatu saa kumi. How can I invite my friends naturally?
Wavuga uti: "Nshuti zanjye, tuzakina umupira w'amaguru ku wa Gatandatu saa kumi. Muzaze dukine!" Ibi birumvikana kandi bifite injyana isanzwe mu butumwa bugufi.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0067-082d6884.

messages.content: Actually, the field is busy then. I want to move the same game to Sunday at saa tatu, not plan a different activity.
Wahindura ubutumwa uti: "Nshuti zanjye, umukino wacu w'umupira w'amaguru wimuriwe ku Cyumweru saa tatu. Muzaze dukine!" "Umukino wacu" makes it clear that this is the same game.

messages.content: Two friends cannot come that early. Let's keep Sunday but change it to saa kumi. How should I revise it?
Vuga uti: "Nshuti zanjye, tuzakina umupira w'amaguru ku Cyumweru saa kumi, si saa tatu. Muzaze dukine!" Umunsi n'igikorwa ntibihindutse; isaha ni yo yahindutse.

messages.content: Soma aka gace: "Aline yanditse inkuru ngufi. Yayisomeye Eric, maze Eric aramwenyura avuga ko inkuru ishimishije. Nyuma, Aline yayihinduye ikinamico." Who wrote the short story?
Aline ni we wanditse inkuru ngufi. The first sentence states this directly: "Aline yanditse inkuru ngufi."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0068-ff12541f.

messages.content: Could I replace ishimishije with irasekeje without changing the meaning?

Oya, si kimwe neza. "Inkuru irashimishije" means the story is enjoyable or pleasing. "Inkuru irasekeje" specifically means the story is funny and makes people laugh. The passage only says that Eric found it enjoyable.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0068-ff12541f.

messages.content: In "Aline yayihinduye ikinamico," what does "it" refer to: the story or the play?
"It" refers to inkuru ngufi, the short story. Aline changed the story into a play, so ikinamico is the result of the change, not the thing represented by -yi- in yayihinduye.

messages.content: Ijambo "inshuti" risobanura iki? Mpa urugero rwerekeye umuryango.
"Inshuti" ni umuntu mufitanye ubucuti. Urugero: "Mushiki wanjye yatumiye inshuti ye ngo dusangire." Aha "inshuti ye" ni umuntu ufitanye ubucuti na mushiki wawe; si ngombwa ko aba uwo mu muryango.

messages.content: None se iyo nshuti ifashije mushiki wanjye gutegura ameza, nabivuga nte?
Wavuga uti: "Iyo nshuti yafashije mushiki wanjye gutegura ameza." Aha "iyo nshuti" yerekeza kuri ya nshuti mushiki wawe yatumiye; ntitwongeyemo undi muntu.

messages.content: Give me a short Kinyarwanda passage about an everyday sports event. Include the exact time and some singular-plural contrasts, then ask me what happened.

Passage: "Ku wa Gatandatu saa tatu za mu gitondo, Kamali yagiye ku kibuga. Yajyanye umupira umwe, ariko ahasanga imipira ibiri n'abakinnyi batandatu. Abakinnyi bakinnye iminota mirongo ine, hanyuma barataha."

Question: What happened at the field, and at what exact time did Kamali go there? Notice these contrasts: "umupira" becomes "imipira," and "umukinnyi" becomes "abakinnyi."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0069-6a4a2d60.

messages.content: Kamali went at 9:00 a.m., found two balls and six players, and they played for forty minutes. Who won the game, and does the passage say who owned the two balls?

Your summary is correct. The passage does not say who won, and it does not say who owned the two balls. It only states: "Ahasanga imipira ibiri n'abakinnyi batandatu." We should not infer the missing details.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0069-6a4a2d60.

messages.content: Please help me say, "I planted one bean and two maize plants in the field," and show me the plural of maize plant.
Say: "Nateye igishyimbo kimwe n'ibigori bibiri mu murima." "Ikigori" is one maize plant, while "ibigori" is the plural. The number word agrees with the plural: "bibiri."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0070-a7b86903.

messages.content: Now how do I say, "I watered them"? By "them," I mean everything I just mentioned.
Say: "Navomereye igishyimbo n'ibigori." Repeating both nouns makes it clear that you watered the bean and the two maize plants.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0070-a7b86903.

messages.content: Then how do I say, "They grew well"? This time "they" means only the two maize plants, not the bean.
Say: "Ibigori bibiri byakuze neza." Here "byakuze" refers specifically to "ibigori bibiri," so the bean is not included.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=multi-turn-consistency. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-multi-turn-consistency-0070-a7b86903.

messages.content: In this short passage, what does the object marker refer to, and how can I tell? "Umuhinzi yabonye ihene mu murima. Bukeye arayigaburira, ariko ntiyasarura ibishyimbo."

In "arayigaburira," the object marker "-yi-" refers to "ihene": "The next day, he feeds it." The nearby noun and the meaning of "feed" support that reading. By contrast, "Umuhinzi arabisarura" means "The farmer harvests them," where "-bi-" refers to plural "ibishyimbo." The form changes because the omitted object changes: "ihene" takes "-yi-," while "ibishyimbo" takes "-bi-." Therefore, the farmer fed the goat but did not harvest the beans.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0071-dfab0813.

messages.content: Mpa umwandiko mugufi uvuga ku byo umuntu akora buri gitondo. Ushiremo interuro isobanura izina, hanyuma umbaze ibibazo bibiri.

Buri gitondo, Keza abyuka saa kumi n'ebyiri, agategura icyayi. Saa moya, ajyana umwana we ku ishuri riri hafi y'aho batuye. Agarutse, asoma ubutumwa bwo ku kazi bwamugezeho nijoro.

Ibibazo: Ishuri riri he? Ubutumwa bwo ku kazi bwageze kuri Keza ryari?

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0071-511b1c60.

messages.content: Mu magambo "ishuri riri hafi y'aho batuye," igice "riri hafi y'aho batuye" gisobanura iki? Kandi se umwandiko uvuga igihe Keza atangirira akazi?

Igice "riri hafi y'aho batuye" gisobanura ishuri Keza ajyanaho umwana: iryo shuri riri hafi y'urugo rwabo. Ubutumwa bwamugezeho nijoro. Igihe Keza atangirira akazi ntikivugwa mu mwandiko, bityo ntitwakimenya dushingiye kuri wo.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0071-511b1c60.

messages.content: Ndashaka kwitoza guha umunyeshuri inzira iva ku irembo ijya ku isomero. Agomba kunyura ku biro by'ubuyobozi, agahindukira ibumoso, maze akagera ku isomero.
Ni byiza. Tangira na "banza," ukoreshe "hanyuma" ku ntambwe ikurikiraho, urangize na "maze." Gerageza gutanga ayo mabwiriza.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0072-f607864b.

messages.content: Banza unyure ibiro by'ubuyobozi, nyuma uhindukire ibumoso, maze ugere ku isomero.
Birumvikana. Kosora "unyure ibiro" uvuge "unyure ku biro." Kandi "hanyuma" irahuza izi ntambwe neza: "Banza unyure ku biro by'ubuyobozi, hanyuma uhindukire ibumoso, maze ugere ku isomero."

messages.content: Banza unyure ku biro by'ubuyobozi, hanyuma uhindukire ibumoso, maze ugere ku isomero.
Ni byo. "Banza," "hanyuma" na "maze" bituma inzira ikurikirana neza kandi ikorohera uyumva.

messages.content: What is the natural English translation of "Buri gitondo mbyuka saa kumi n'ebyiri, ariko mpaguruka nyuma y'iminota itanu"? I am practicing kubyuka and guhaguruka.
"Every morning I wake up at six, but I get up five minutes later." Here mbyuka means "I wake up," while mpaguruka means "I get up" or rise from where I was lying or sitting.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0073-cb238e43.

messages.content: How about "Uyu munsi nabyutse kare, ariko nahagurutse nyuma"?
It means: "Today I woke up early, but I got up later." Nabyutse describes becoming awake; nahagurutse describes actually getting up, so the same distinction is preserved.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0073-cb238e43.

messages.content: Nari navuze iby'isupu, hanyuma ndavuga nti: "Maze gukata imboga, nayishyize mu isafuriya." I sound unnatural when I mean that I put the vegetables into the pot. Why?

Because "nayishyize" means "I put it," its "-yi-" naturally points back to singular "isupu," not plural "imboga." To preserve your meaning, say: "Maze gukata imboga, nazishyize mu isafuriya." Here "-zi-" points back to "imboga." Interuro yuzuye kandi isanzwe ni: "Maze gukata imboga, nazishyize mu isafuriya."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=learner-correction-feedback. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-learner-correction-feedback-0073-dd92d072.

messages.content: Translate into Kinyarwanda: "Those two stories are beautiful, but I do not know who wrote them, so I will not invent the writers' names." Please explain the plural forms.

Izo nkuru ebyiri ni nziza, ariko sinzi abazanditse, bityo sinzahimba amazina y'abanditsi.

Although inkuru has the same written form in the singular and plural, izo, ebyiri, and nziza all show that it is plural. Sinzahimba preserves "I will not invent" and avoids claiming an unknown fact.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0074-7f8d1ee7.

messages.content: Ejo jye na Aline twagiye mu mujyi. Twateze bisi, ariko yaratinze, turayitegereza. Nabivuga nte mu ncamake nkoresheje insimburazina?
Vuga uti: "Ejo jye na Aline twagiye mu mujyi na bisi. Yo yaratinze, ariko twe twarayitegereje." "Jye" yerekana uvuga, "twe" ikagaruka kuri wowe na Aline, naho "yo" ikagaruka kuri bisi.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0074-8147467e.

messages.content: Nshobora no kuvuga nti: "Twebwe twayitegereje, hanyuma yo iraza"?
Yego. "Twebwe twayitegereje, hanyuma iraza" birumvikana kandi birasanzwe. "Yo" ushobora kuyongeramo ushaka gushimangira bisi: "hanyuma yo iraza."

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0074-8147467e.

messages.content: Natekerezaga ko "ibiti byacu" means "their trees." Is that correct?

Oya. Ibiti byacu means "our trees." For example: Tugomba kurinda ibiti byacu. means "We must protect our trees." To say "their trees," use ibiti byabo: Bagomba kurinda ibiti byabo. The possessive changes from byacu to byabo while agreeing with the plural noun ibiti.

messages.content: Buri gitondo, noza amenyo wanjye mbere yo kurya. Kuki iyi nteruro yumvikana nabi?
Vuga uti: "Buri gitondo, noza amenyo yanjye mbere yo kurya." Amenyo ni menshi, bityo hano dukoresha yanjye, aho gukoresha wanjye.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0075-a0490f21.

messages.content: None se navuga nti: "Nyuma yo kurya, mfata urugendo rwanjye" nshaka kuvuga ko njya gutembera?
Birumvikana, ariko ntibisanzwe kuvugwa gutyo. Vuga uti: "Nyuma yo kurya, ndatembera." Gufata urugendo rwanjye byumvikanisha urugendo rwawe bwite, si igikorwa gisanzwe cyo gutembera.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=conversation-practice. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-conversation-practice-0075-a0490f21.

messages.content: Hindura mu Cyongereza uti: "Jya ku cyapa cya bisi, hanyuma utegereze bisi ijya ku isoko." Ese "jya" na "genda" bisobanura kimwe hano?

Translation: "Go to the bus stop, then wait for the bus going to the market." Hano "jya" ni yo isanzwe kuko yerekana aho umuntu agana. "Genda" na yo ishobora kuvuga "go," ariko akenshi ishimangira kugenda cyangwa kuva aho uri; si yo ihuye neza n'aya mabwiriza magufi.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=translation-with-explanation. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-translation-with-explanation-0076-b3d1ea6e.

messages.content:
Soma aka gace, hanyuma usubize ibibazo.

Aline yagiye mu murima wa se saa moya za mu gitondo. Yateye ibishyimbo mu gice cy'umurima kiri hafi y'urugo. Nyuma yasubije isuka ya se mu nzu y'ibikoresho.

Aline yagiye mu murima ryari? Umurima wari uwa nde? Yateye iki? Ibishyimbo byari bifite ibara irihe?

Aline yagiye mu murima saa moya za mu gitondo. Umurima wari uwa se, kandi yateye ibishyimbo. Ibara ry'ibishyimbo ntirivugwa muri aka gace, bityo ntidushobora kurimenya. Amagambo wa se na ya se agaragaza ko umurima n'isuka byari ibya se wa Aline.

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=reading-comprehension. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-reading-comprehension-0076-2e150dcc.

messages.content: Ejo nabajije inshuti yanjye nti: "Wabyutse saa ngahe?" Ubu ndashaka kubaza umwarimu icyo kibazo mu cyubahiro. Ni irihe tandukaniro?

Ku nshuti imwe uvuga uti: "Wabyutse saa ngahe? Ujya ku kazi ryari?" Ku mwarimu uvuga uti: "Mwabyutse saa ngahe? Mujya ku kazi ryari?" Mu mvugo y'icyubahiro, "w-" ihinduka "mw-," naho "u-" igahinduka "mu-." Bityo wakomeza uti: "Mwarimu, mwabyutse saa ngahe uyu munsi?"

reviewer_notes: Reviewer: Tessy Mugisha. Basis: original (reviewer flagged Keep; critic revision declined). task_family=grammar-and-structure. conversation_id=sft-distill-production-grammar-and-structure-0077-8517d32b.

messages.content: Mpa agace gato ko gusoma ku buzima bwo mu cyaro, hanyuma umbaze isaha umuhinzi yagiye mu murima n'uwamuherekeje. Niba uwo muntu atavuzwe, ubinsobanurire.

Dore agace: "Ku wa Kabiri, Kamana yagiye muri uyu murima saa moya za mu gitondo. Yuhira ibi biti by'imbuto, hanyuma asubira mu rugo." Ibibazo: Kamana yagiye mu murima saa ngahe? Ni nde wamuherekeje? Ibisubizo: Yagiyeyo saa moya za mu gitondo. Uwamuherekeje ntiyavuzwe muri aka gace, bityo ntitwabimenya dushingiye ku mwandiko.

messages.content: Mu kiganiro nanditse nti: "Aline abyuka saa kumi n'ebyiri. Beata ategura icyayi. Nyuma y'ibyo, arakaraba." Which is clearer if I mean Aline: "Aline arakaraba" or "na we arakaraba"?

"Aline arakaraba" is clearer. In your original version, arakaraba could refer to Aline or Beata because both are people already mentioned. "Na we arakaraba" means "she also washes," but it still depends on context to identify her. Compare:

Aline arakaraba. - Aline washes.
Na we arakaraba. - She washes too.
